content.problemStatement:
JARVIS needs help managing meeting rooms at Stark Tower. Given N meetings with their start and end times, determine the minimum number of conference rooms required so that all meetings can be conducted without overlap. If a meeting ends at time X, another meeting can start at time X using the same room.


Input Format

First line contains TTT, number of test cases
For each test case:

First line contains NNN, number of meetings
Second line contains NNN space-separated integers denoting start times
Third line contains NNN space-separated integers denoting end times




Output Format

For each test case, print the minimum number of conference rooms needed.
constraints: All times are given in 24‑hour integer format
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case:
- The first line contains an integer N, the number of meetings.
- The second line contains N space‑separated integers, the start times of the meetings.
- The third line contains N space‑separated integers, the end times of the meetings. A meeting that ends at time X frees the room for another meeting that starts at time X.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the minimum number of conference rooms required.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Meetings are [1300‑1600], [1500‑1700], [1600‑1800]. At 1300 one room is used, at 1500 a second room is needed, at 1600 the first meeting ends and the third can reuse that room. Maximum concurrent meetings = 2.

solution.approach: Sort the start times and the end times separately. Then walk through both arrays with two pointers. If the next meeting starts before the earliest ending meeting finishes (start[i] < end[j]) we need an extra room, so increment the current room count and move the start pointer. Otherwise a meeting has finished, we can reuse a room: decrement the current room count and move the end pointer. The maximum value of the current room count during the walk is the answer. This works because sorting aligns meetings in chronological order and the two‑pointer scan always reflects the exact number of ongoing meetings at any moment.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case due to sorting the start and end arrays.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the two sorted arrays (or in‑place sorting).
